scope_note: Primary scope: RISC-V processor fuzzing, multi-hart/memory-system verification, microarchitectural security testing, and RTL/SoC hardware fuzzing. General software, firmware, compiler, GPU-kernel, and unrelated AI-security papers are excluded unless they directly evaluate processor hardware or RTL.
queries: (all:"RISC-V" OR all:riscv) AND (all:fuzzing OR all:"differential testing" OR all:"random testing")
(all:processor OR all:CPU) AND (all:fuzzing OR all:"coverage-guided") AND (all:RTL OR all:hardware)
(all:RTL OR all:Verilog OR all:SystemVerilog) AND (all:fuzzing OR all:"directed testing")
(all:"multi-hart" OR all:multihart OR all:multicore) AND (all:fuzzing OR all:verification OR all:testing)
(all:"cache coherence" OR all:coherency) AND (all:verification OR all:"test generation" OR all:fuzzing)
(all:"memory consistency" OR all:RVWMO OR all:litmus) AND (all:verification OR all:checker OR all:fuzzing)
(all:TileLink OR all:ProbeAck OR all:AcquireBlock OR all:GrantData) AND (all:verification OR all:bug OR all:fuzzing)
(all:microarchitectural OR all:microarchitecture) AND (all:fuzzing OR all:"automated testing")
(all:"transient execution" OR all:"speculative execution") AND (all:fuzzing OR all:"automated detection")
(all:"timing vulnerability" OR all:"microarchitectural leakage") AND (all:fuzzing OR all:testing)
(all:"hardware sanitizer" OR all:"information flow tracking" OR all:taint) AND (all:processor OR all:RTL)
(all:"bug injection" OR all:mutant OR all:"mutation testing") AND (all:processor OR all:RTL OR all:hardware)

google_scholar_note.reason: Google Scholar has no stable public API and automated scraping often triggers CAPTCHA. Use it manually for forward/backward citation chasing, or use a paid provider such as SerpAPI.
